title: trusted blocks can assert Send+Sync on \!Sync types enabling data races
description: trusted('...') allows programmers to assert type-system properties the compiler cannot verify. This includes asserting that a \!Sync type implements Sync, which defeats the data-race safety model. Example: Arc<Atomic<RefCell<u64>>> can be made Send+Sync via trusted, then shared across threads, causing a data race on RefCell which is not Sync. Send and Sync bounds are Ferrum's primary concurrency safety guarantee. Making them bypassable with a named string assertion reduces them to advisory documentation. The trusted keyword must be prohibited from asserting Send or Sync on types containing \!Sync fields — or the language must abandon its data-race-free claim.
close_reason: Fixed in ferrum-lang-verification.md (§3.3) and ferrum-lang-concurrency.md (§1.10). trusted is now prohibited from asserting Send/Sync on types with \!Sync fields — compile error. The compiler's structural \!Sync determination cannot be overridden by trusted. Legitimate use case (implementing a custom sync primitive) redirected to unsafe impl Sync. Design decision documented inline.
